aids.title: Heap Operations: ISD
aids.content: I = Insert (add at end, sift UP), S = Search-min/max (look at root, O(1)), D = Delete-min/max (swap root with last, sift DOWN). Remember: Insert goes UP, Delete goes DOWN.
aids.forRemembering: The direction of element movement in heap insert and delete operations

aids.title: DEPQ as a Hospital ER
aids.content: A double-ended priority queue is like a hospital ER triage system. The most critical patient (max priority) is treated first, while the least critical patient (min priority) can be discharged. The system needs quick access to both extremes -- just like a DEPQ provides O(1) access to both min and max.
aids.forRemembering: Why double-ended priority queues need efficient access to both min and max

aids.title: PQ Implementation Complexity Comparison
aids.content: Unsorted array: Insert O(1), Delete O(n). Sorted array: Insert O(n), Delete O(1). Binary heap: Insert O(log n), Delete O(log n). The heap is the balanced choice -- both operations are logarithmic. Always prefer heaps for priority queues in practice.
aids.forRemembering: Time complexity comparison of different priority queue implementations
